behavior_summary: U8P_AFTERNEC::hatch runs as a process that records that the player has "talkedToDeadDudes" and then removes the item instance that ran the process.
The script's effect is a one-shot state update (set global flag) followed by cleanup (Item::destroy on the current item).
There is no dialogue, branching, menu interaction, or parameterized behavior in this script; behavior is deterministic and linear.
notable_control_flow: Linear sequence: set global flag talkedToDeadDudes = true, then call Item::destroy(this).
No conditional branches, loops, or UI/menu interactions are present.
open_questions: Exactly what game event starts the processType 0x0210 'U8P_AFTERNEC::hatch' (e.g., item use, timer expiry, or lifecycle completion)?
How is the global flag talkedToDeadDudes consumed elsewhere (which dialogues/quests or guards check it)?
Is destroying the item intended to prevent repeated triggering, or is the item already transient (i.e., is this the only cleanup path)?

main_function.name: U8P_AFTERNEC::hatch
main_function.purpose: Mark that the player has interacted with the "dead dudes" (set talkedToDeadDudes) and then destroy the item instance that ran this process to perform cleanup.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the item-level process 'U8P_AFTERNEC::hatch' runs (processType 0x0210) — likely on item lifecycle completion, use, or a related event; exact trigger is inferred.

function_map.name: U8P_AFTERNEC::hatch
function_map.role: record interaction state and cleanup (sets flag, destroys item)

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: engine-provided cleanup call to remove/destroy the current item instance

external_calls.purpose: Destroy the current item instance (this) to remove it from the world after recording the interaction.

writes.name: talkedToDeadDudes
writes.likely_effect: Set to true to record that the player completed/acknowledged the interaction with the 'dead dudes', likely gating subsequent dialogue or quest state.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_AFTERNEC::hatch runs as a process that records that the player has "talkedToDeadDudes" and then removes the item instance that ran the process.
